diagrams.description: Local gate outcomes are promoted into the canonical ledger and bound to immutable delivery facts; incomplete promotion fails closed into reconciliation.
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  L[Local gate outcomes] --> P[Canonical promotion]
  P --> B[Decision outcome binding]
  D[Immutable delivery] --> B
  B -->|complete| C[Canonical audit revision]
  B -->|incomplete| R[Reconciliation required]

clauses.statement: Stable source keys produce deterministic trace identities; ambiguous or keyless sources retain null identities with deterministic collision selectors and are never deduplicated away.

test_refs.case: GDL-S-1 stable trace identity is source/order independent and missing keys stay explicit

clauses.statement: Finding, gate, detector, disposition, and decision claims retain per-value provenance and conflicting authoritative claims remain explicit.

test_refs.case: GDL-S-2 every decision-chain value retains its own source provenance

clauses.statement: Behavior deltas retain before/after references and accept current behavior only from verification bound to the current content authority.

test_refs.case: GDL-S-3 behavior delta only accepts explicit values from a matching current command

clauses.statement: PR and merge authority update the same trace revision while mismatched delivery identities remain explicit conflicts.

test_refs.case: GDL-S-4 matching delivery identity is refreshed from current authority instead of stale ledger fields

clauses.statement: Downstream outcome remains tri-state and unreadable, untrusted, or mismatched sources stay explicit instead of becoming success, failure, or zero value.

test_refs.case: GDL-S-5 observation resolution selects the exact parent revision and preserves tri-state trust errors

clauses.statement: Review, PR preparation, and usage reporting expose the same deterministic bounded decision chain with counts, selectors, and a path and digest back to the full ledger.

test_refs.case: GDL-S-6 common summary is bounded, deterministic, and reconstructs a redacted decision chain

clauses.statement: Legacy ledgers remain readable, known legacy promotion sources become explicit empty sources, and malformed or unknown sources fail closed without breaking existing consumers.

test_refs.case: GDL-S-7 known v1 and v2 ledgers remain readable as legacy empty promotion sources

clauses.statement: Canonical revisions deduplicate identical bytes, preserve distinct revisions, validate all target paths, bound external commands, and fail closed when required Git identities or push postconditions are unavailable.

test_refs.case: GDL-CONTRACT-008 fails closed when required Git identity probes are unavailable

clauses.statement: Outcome record, refresh, and canonical persistence validate the Story ID before any ledger or temporary-worktree path construction, then validate story-scoped selectors, parent revision, producer, managed authority, live delivery, canonical base, atomic local writes, and explicit reconciliation after remote application but local finalization failure.

test_refs.case: GDL-S-9 refresh finalization preserves prior ledger bytes when atomic replacement fails
GDL-S-9 canonical persistence rejects traversal story IDs before constructing a worktree path

test_pattern.must_cover: GDL-S-9 canonical persistence rejects traversal story IDs

clauses.statement: Canonical promotion deterministically stores every local decision outcome in the central ledger revision.
A binding is bound only when promoted_count plus duplicate_count equals the expected local entry count.
The delivery binding workflow transition preserves immutable delivery and records reconciliation_required with decision_outcome_binding_failed when promotion is incomplete.
A story with zero local decision outcomes produces a not_applicable binding and never invents a missing-outcome failure.
Best-effort decision_outcome_delivery and fail-closed decision_outcome_binding remain separate fields and failure contracts.
Outcome recording continues to require an authoritative delivery base.
A caller-selected base continues to be rejected when it differs from the authoritative delivery base.
Session-backed evidence reuse remains conditional on at least one explicitly attributed session.
When delivery-efficiency policy is enabled, an agent review result remains recordable only for a lifecycle started through a consumed dispatch authorization.
